dialogues.speaker: Robin

content_blocks.scene_description: A story about Robin using his new BDS feature to help Wu Binbin and Mike find the Italian restaurant.

key_points.chinese: 罗宾有北斗卫星导航系统！

key_points.english: He can help the boys find the Italian restaurant.
key_points.chinese: 他可以帮助男孩们找到意大利餐厅。

intro_text.question: You are in a car. Which of these can help you find a place?

paragraphs.english: Robin has BDS! Wu Binbin's grandpa gave Robin a new feature. He now has BDS. He can help the boys find the Italian restaurant.
paragraphs.chinese: 罗宾有北斗卫星导航系统！吴彬彬的爷爷给了罗宾一个新功能。他现在有北斗卫星导航系统。他可以帮助男孩们找到意大利餐厅。

dialogues.speaker: Robin
dialogues.english: We're in front of the cinema. Let's go straight and turn left at the bookstore. Follow me, please!
dialogues.chinese: 我们在电影院前面。让我们直走，在书店左转。请跟我来！

dialogues.speaker: Mike
dialogues.english: Is it far?

dialogues.speaker: Robin
dialogues.english: No. Now we are behind the hospital. Let's turn right and then turn right again.
dialogues.chinese: 不。现在我们在医院后面。让我们右转，然后再右转。

dialogues.speaker: Mike
dialogues.english: There is the restaurant!

dialogues.speaker: Robin
dialogues.english: My BDS works!
dialogues.chinese: 我的北斗卫星导航系统奏效了！

dialogues.speaker: Wu Binbin
dialogues.english: Yes! I'll tell Grandpa. But let's eat first. I'm so hungry!
dialogues.chinese: 是的！我会告诉爷爷。但让我们先吃饭吧。我太饿了！

content_blocks.scene_description: Zoom and Zip are in London. Zoom wants to buy fish and chips.

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: Hey, that looks tasty. Where can I buy one?
dialogues.chinese: 嘿，那个看起来很好吃。我在哪里可以买到？

dialogues.speaker: Boy
dialogues.english: Near the London Eye. Go that way.
dialogues.chinese: 在伦敦眼附近。往那边走。

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: Excuse me. Where is the London Eye?
dialogues.chinese: 打扰一下。伦敦眼在哪里？

dialogues.speaker: Man
dialogues.english: It's next to the film museum near the Thames.
dialogues.chinese: 它在泰晤士河附近的电影博物馆旁边。

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: Excuse me. Is the Thames far from here?
dialogues.chinese: 打扰一下。泰晤士河离这儿远吗？

dialogues.speaker: Man
dialogues.english: No. Go straight and turn left.

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: Zip, look!

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: Finally!

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: Hi! Three large portions and one small one, please.
dialogues.chinese: 嗨！请给我三份大的和一份小的。

dialogues.speaker: Man
dialogues.english: OK.

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: Oh, my stomach hurts! I miss my tanghulu!
dialogues.chinese: 哦，我的肚子疼！我想念我的糖葫芦！

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: You still want to eat?!

Zoom finding the Fish & Chips shop

content_blocks.scene_description: Wu Binbin asks Mr Jones for directions to Fuxing Hospital. They discuss bikes, helmets, and traffic rules while looking at photos.

key_points.english: How can I get to the Fuxing Hospital?
Take the No. 57 bus over there.
In the USA people on bikes must wear one.
key_points.chinese: 在美国，骑自行车的人必须戴头盔。

key_points.english: Don't go at the red light!
I must pay attention to the traffic lights!
key_points.chinese: 我必须注意交通信号灯！

dialogues.speaker: Wu Binbin
dialogues.english: Mr Jones, how can I get to the Fuxing Hospital?
dialogues.chinese: 琼斯先生，我怎么去复兴医院？

dialogues.speaker: Mr Jones
dialogues.english: Take the No. 57 bus over there.

dialogues.speaker: Wu Binbin
dialogues.english: Thanks. Wow! So many pictures of bikes!
dialogues.chinese: 谢谢。哇！这么多自行车的照片！

dialogues.speaker: Mr Jones
dialogues.english: They're from my cousin in the USA.
dialogues.chinese: 它们来自我在美国的表弟。

dialogues.speaker: Wu Binbin
dialogues.english: What's this?

dialogues.speaker: Mr Jones
dialogues.english: A helmet. In the USA people on bikes must wear one.
dialogues.chinese: 头盔。在美国，骑自行车的人必须戴头盔。

dialogues.speaker: Wu Binbin
dialogues.english: I see. Oh, the bus is coming! Bye, Mr Jones.
dialogues.chinese: 我明白了。哦，公共汽车来了！再见，琼斯先生。

dialogues.speaker: Mr Jones
dialogues.english: Hey, don't go at the red light!

dialogues.speaker: Wu Binbin
dialogues.english: Oh, right! Thanks. I must pay attention to the traffic lights!
dialogues.chinese: 哦，对！谢谢。我必须注意交通信号灯！

paragraphs.english: Some children go to school on foot in Munich, Germany.
paragraphs.chinese: 在德国慕尼黑，一些孩子步行去上学。

paragraphs.english: In Alaska, USA, it snows a lot. Some kids go to school by sled. It's fast.
paragraphs.chinese: 在美国阿拉斯加，下雪很多。一些孩子乘雪橇去上学。它很快。

paragraphs.chinese: 在中国北京，一些孩子每天乘地铁去上学。

paragraphs.english: In Papa Westray, Scotland, the children go to school by ferry. But in 2009, they went to school by plane because the ferry didn't work.
paragraphs.chinese: 在苏格兰的帕帕韦斯特雷，孩子们乘轮渡去上学。但在2009年，他们乘飞机去上学，因为轮渡无法运作。

content_blocks.scene_description: Amy meets her friend Annie from the UK. They discuss the differences between China and the UK regarding food, traffic rules, and buses.

key_points.english: Chinese food is different from British food.
key_points.chinese: 中国食物与英国食物不同。

key_points.english: In China, we must look left and then right before crossing the road.
key_points.chinese: 在中国，过马路前我们必须先向左看，再向右看。

key_points.english: In China, bus doors are on the right side.
key_points.chinese: 在中国，公交车门在右侧。

dialogues.speaker: Amy
dialogues.english: It's so good to see you, Annie!

dialogues.speaker: Annie
dialogues.english: You too, Amy. I missed you.

dialogues.speaker: Annie
dialogues.english: Wow, Chinese food is so different from British food.
dialogues.chinese: 哇，中国食物和英国食物太不一样了。

dialogues.speaker: Amy
dialogues.english: Yes. Can you use chopsticks?

dialogues.speaker: Annie
dialogues.english: No, I can't.

dialogues.speaker: Amy
dialogues.english: Let's take a bus home. We must cross the road.
dialogues.chinese: 我们坐公交车回家吧。我们必须穿过马路。

dialogues.speaker: Annie
dialogues.english: I see. We must look right before crossing the road.
dialogues.chinese: 我明白了。我们在穿过马路前必须向右看。

dialogues.speaker: Amy
dialogues.english: No, first left and then right!
dialogues.chinese: 不，先向左看，然后向右看！

dialogues.speaker: Amy
dialogues.english: Look, the bus is over there!

dialogues.speaker: Annie
dialogues.english: Wow, it's a double-decker! The same as in London.
dialogues.chinese: 哇，是双层巴士！和伦敦的一样。

dialogues.speaker: Annie
dialogues.english: Where is the door? Amy, there is no door on the bus!
dialogues.chinese: 门在哪里？艾米，这辆公交车上没有门！

dialogues.speaker: Amy
dialogues.english: Haha, you're looking at the wrong side again!

content_blocks.scene_description: A diary entry written by Wu Binbin about his plans for the Mid-Autumn Festival, including an acrostic poem about FAMILY.

key_points.english: My family are going to get together and have a big dinner.
key_points.chinese: 我的家人打算聚在一起吃顿大餐。

key_points.english: My aunt is going to make mooncakes.
We will all be together tonight.
key_points.chinese: 今晚我们将都聚在一起。

paragraphs.english: Sunday
paragraphs.chinese: 星期日

paragraphs.english: Dear Diary,
paragraphs.chinese: 亲爱的日记，

paragraphs.english: Tomorrow is Mid-Autumn Festival. My family are going to get together and have a big dinner. My aunt is going to make mooncakes. My grandma will tell us a story about Chang'e. Robin and I are going to read a poem. This is our poem:
paragraphs.chinese: 明天是中秋节。我的家人打算聚在一起吃顿大餐。我的姑姑打算做月饼。我的奶奶将给我们讲一个关于嫦娥的故事。罗宾和我打算读一首诗。这是我们的诗：

paragraphs.english: F is for family. We will all be together tonight.
paragraphs.chinese: F代表家庭。今晚我们将都聚在一起。

paragraphs.english: A is for autumn. It is the autumn season.
paragraphs.chinese: A代表秋天。现在是秋季。

paragraphs.english: M is for moon. We eat mooncakes and tell stories about the moon.
paragraphs.chinese: M代表月亮。我们吃月饼并讲关于月亮的故事。

paragraphs.english: I is for "I". I am so happy today.
paragraphs.chinese: I代表“我”。我今天很开心。

paragraphs.english: L is for love. We love Mid-Autumn Festival.
paragraphs.chinese: L代表爱。我们爱中秋节。

paragraphs.english: Y is for you. You can be together with your family too!
paragraphs.chinese: Y代表你。你也可以和你的家人聚在一起！

image_captions: Robin playing a musical instrument (lute/pipa)

content_blocks.scene_description: Zoom plans to learn how to swim but tries to do it by watching TV. Zip encourages him to go to the pool and learn by practice.

key_points.english: What are you going to do tomorrow?
I'm going to learn how to swim.

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: What are you going to do tomorrow?

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: I'm going to learn how to swim.

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: OK. I'll teach you.

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: Please don't disturb me. I'm learning to swim.
dialogues.chinese: 请不要打扰我。我正在学游泳。

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: How can you learn to swim without going to a pool?
dialogues.chinese: 不去游泳池你怎么能学会游泳呢？

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: Come on. Let's go to the swimming pool.

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: No! I'm afraid of water.

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: Just jump in! Catch this. Practise and you will learn.
dialogues.chinese: 跳进去就行！抓住这个。练习你就会学会。

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: Help! Help!

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: Just try. This way ... This way ... OK, now can you do it?
dialogues.chinese: 试一试。这边……这边……好了，现在你会了吗？

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: Oh, it's easy.

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: We should always remember: "Learn by doing."
dialogues.chinese: 我们要永远记住：“从做中学。”

content_blocks.scene_description: The little boy is waiting for his mother. Robin is singing a song to him.

paragraphs.english: The little boy is waiting for his mother. Robin is singing a song to him. Listen and read the song aloud.
paragraphs.chinese: 小男孩正在等他的妈妈。罗宾正在给他唱一首歌。听录音并大声朗读这首歌。

paragraphs.english: Hush, little boy, don't say a word.
paragraphs.chinese: 嘘，小男孩，别说话。

paragraphs.english: Papa's going to buy you a little bird.
paragraphs.chinese: 爸爸打算给你买一只小鸟。

paragraphs.english: And if that bird doesn't sing,
paragraphs.chinese: 如果那只鸟不唱歌，

paragraphs.english: Papa's going to buy you a big, big ring.
paragraphs.chinese: 爸爸打算给你买一个大大的戒指。

paragraphs.english: And if that ring is lost in class,
paragraphs.chinese: 如果那个戒指在课上丢了，

paragraphs.english: Papa's going to buy you a looking glass.
paragraphs.chinese: 爸爸打算给你买一面镜子。

paragraphs.english: And if that looking glass falls down,
paragraphs.chinese: 如果那面镜子掉下来，

paragraphs.english: Well, you'll still be the sweetest boy in town.
paragraphs.chinese: 好吧，你仍然是镇上最可爱的男孩。

content_blocks.scene_description: A story about a squirrel named Zac and the Monkey King, explaining why squirrels have bushy tails.

key_points.english: He likes singing and dancing very much.
key_points.chinese: 他非常喜欢唱歌和跳舞。

key_points.english: The Monkey King likes reading books.
Zac liked his new tail, and so did all the other squirrels.
key_points.chinese: 扎克喜欢他的新尾巴，其他所有的松鼠也喜欢。

paragraphs.english: Zac lives in a forest. He likes singing and dancing very much.
paragraphs.chinese: 扎克住在森林里。他非常喜欢唱歌和跳舞。

paragraphs.english: Every day the squirrels have to give the Monkey King gifts. Then the Monkey King lets them get food from his forest.
paragraphs.chinese: 每天松鼠们必须给猴王送礼物。然后猴王才让他们从他的森林里获取食物。

paragraphs.english: Zac sometimes sings and dances in front of the king. The Monkey King likes reading books, so he doesn't like Zac's music.
paragraphs.chinese: 扎克有时在猴王面前唱歌跳舞。猴王喜欢看书，所以他不喜欢扎克的音乐。

paragraphs.english: One day, Zac sang for the king. The king was very angry and shouted, "Go away!" Then he threw Zac out of the tree.
paragraphs.chinese: 有一天，扎克为猴王唱歌。猴王非常生气并大喊：“走开！”然后他把扎克扔出了树。

paragraphs.english: Zac got up and looked at his tail. It was really bushy! Zac liked his new tail, and so did all the other squirrels. So from that day on all squirrels' tails were bushy.
paragraphs.chinese: 扎克站起来看了看他的尾巴。它真的很浓密！扎克喜欢他的新尾巴，其他所有的松鼠也一样。所以从那天起，所有松鼠的尾巴都是浓密的。

dialogues.speaker: Squirrel
dialogues.english: What a great story! Is it true?
dialogues.chinese: 多么棒的故事啊！是真的吗？

content_blocks.scene_description: Zoom and Zip are watching TV and discussing various unusual jobs.

focus_vocabulary.chinese: 剥坚果的人（双关语：胡桃夹子）

key_points.english: What other unusual jobs can you think of?
key_points.chinese: 你还能想到什么其他不寻常的工作？

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: Wow! What a great job!

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: Yes. What other unusual jobs can you think of?
dialogues.chinese: 是的。你还能想到什么其他不寻常的工作？

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: How about a lion tamer?

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: Oh, no. Too dangerous!

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: What about a bee farmer? I love honey.
dialogues.chinese: 养蜂人怎么样？我喜欢蜂蜜。

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: Hmm. Maybe a bee farmer will get stung. That's not for me.
dialogues.chinese: 嗯。也许养蜂人会被蛰。那不适合我。

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: Maybe you can be a computer game tester. You can play games and work at home.
dialogues.chinese: 也许你可以当一名电脑游戏测试员。你可以玩游戏并且在家里工作。

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: I like to study. I don't like computer games.
dialogues.chinese: 我喜欢学习。我不喜欢电脑游戏。

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: What about a magician's assistant? That would be cool.
dialogues.chinese: 魔术师助手怎么样？那会很酷。

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: Oh, no. I don't like that.

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: Well, what do you want to be?

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: I want to be a nut cracker. Yum! I like eating nuts.
dialogues.chinese: 我想做一个剥坚果的人。美味！我喜欢吃坚果。

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: Haha! You're nuts!
dialogues.chinese: 哈哈！你疯了！（双关：你真逗！）

dialogues.english: Yum! I'm so happy. I love popcorn!

dialogues.speaker: Sarah
dialogues.english: Here you are. I'll go and get some drinks. Wait for me.
dialogues.chinese: 给你。我去拿点饮料。等等我。

dialogues.english: Hey, where's my popcorn?

dialogues.speaker: Sam
dialogues.english: Yum! It was so good.

dialogues.speaker: Sarah
dialogues.english: What?! How could you eat all the popcorn?! We should share!
dialogues.chinese: 什么？！你怎么能把爆米花全吃光了？！我们应该分享！

content_blocks.scene_description: Sarah asks her mother for advice on how to handle anger and illness in two different scenes: the kitchen and the bedroom.

key_points.english: If I feel angry, what should I do?
key_points.chinese: 如果我感到生气，我该怎么做？

dialogues.speaker: Sarah
dialogues.english: Mum, if I feel angry, what should I do?
dialogues.chinese: 妈妈，如果我感到生气，我该怎么做？

dialogues.speaker: Mum
dialogues.english: Well, first, take a deep breath. Then you should count to ten.
dialogues.chinese: 嗯，首先，深呼吸。然后你应该数到十。

dialogues.speaker: Sarah
dialogues.english: After that ...?

dialogues.speaker: Mum
dialogues.english: After that, you won't feel so angry.
dialogues.chinese: 在那之后，你就不会感到那么生气了。

dialogues.speaker: Sarah
dialogues.english: Mum, I feel ill. Should I count to ten, too?
dialogues.chinese: 妈妈，我感觉不舒服。我也应该数到十吗？

dialogues.speaker: Mum
dialogues.english: No, dear. You should see a doctor.
dialogues.chinese: 不，亲爱的。你应该去看医生。

image_captions: Sarah talking to her mum in the kitchen about feeling angry
Sarah lying in bed feeling ill and talking to her mum

content_blocks.scene_description: A story about Robin and an ant. Robin avoids sitting on an ant, and later the ant helps Robin when he is stuck in the mud.

key_points.english: He is stuck in the mud.

paragraphs.english: It is a sunny morning. Robin is going to sit on the grass when he hears, "Wait!"
paragraphs.chinese: 这是一个晴朗的早晨。罗宾正要坐在草地上，这时他听到：“等一等！”

paragraphs.english: It is a little ant. He is afraid. "Please don't sit on me. One day I can help you."
paragraphs.chinese: 是一只小蚂蚁。他很害怕。“请不要坐在我身上。有一天我会帮你的。”

paragraphs.english: "Don't worry, little ant. I won't sit on you," says Robin.
paragraphs.chinese: “别担心，小蚂蚁。我不会坐在你身上的，”罗宾说。

paragraphs.english: The next day, it is raining. Robin is in the park. He is stuck in the mud. He is worried.
paragraphs.chinese: 第二天，下雨了。罗宾在公园里。他陷在泥里了。他很担心。

paragraphs.english: Then he hears, "Let us help you!" It is the ant and all of his friends.
paragraphs.chinese: 这时他听到：“让我们来帮你！”是那只蚂蚁和他所有的朋友们。

paragraphs.english: They are strong. They pull Robin out of the mud.
paragraphs.chinese: 他们很强壮。他们把罗宾从泥里拉了出来。

paragraphs.english: Everyone is happy!
paragraphs.chinese: 大家都很快乐！

content_blocks.scene_description: Zip wants to watch a TV show and eat popcorn. Zoom goes to make some, but he actually plants popcorn seeds in the garden and waits for them to grow, which makes Zip surprised and frustrated.

key_points.english: Count to ten and take a deep breath.

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: My favourite TV show is on soon. Let's watch it. Do you have any popcorn?
dialogues.chinese: 我最喜欢的电视节目马上就要开始了。我们看吧。你有爆米花吗？

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: I'll go and make some.

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: I can't wait. I love popcorn. I'm so happy!
dialogues.chinese: 我等不及了。我爱爆米花。我太高兴了！

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: He must be making lots of popcorn. Yum!
dialogues.chinese: 他肯定在做很多爆米花。美味！

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: Where is he? I'm a little worried now. I'll go and check.
dialogues.chinese: 他在哪里？我现在有点担心。我去看看。

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: Oh! Zoom, what are you doing here?

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: I'm making popcorn.

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: What?!

dialogues.speaker: Zoom
dialogues.english: Oh, don't be angry! Count to ten and take a deep breath. Well, I planted the popcorn seeds. But they take a long time to grow. I'm still waiting.
dialogues.chinese: 噢，别生气！数到十，深呼吸。那个，我种下了爆米花种子。但是它们需要很长时间才能长大。我还在等呢。

dialogues.speaker: Zip
dialogues.english: Oooooh noooo!

content_blocks.scene_description: A story about a rich businessman testing the honesty of three men using flower seeds.

key_points.english: The man with the most beautiful flower can have my money!
key_points.chinese: 拥有最美丽花朵的人可以得到我的钱！

key_points.english: The seeds are bad and will never sprout!
key_points.chinese: 种子是坏的，永远不会发芽！

paragraphs.english: One day, a rich old businessman in a small town meets a factory worker, a coach and a fisherman. He gives them three seeds and says, "Bring me the flowers in three months. The man with the most beautiful flower can have my money!"
paragraphs.chinese: 一天，一个小镇上有钱的老商人遇见了一个工厂工人、一个教练和一个渔夫。他给了他们三颗种子并说：“三个月后把花带给我。拥有最美丽花朵的人可以得到我的钱！”

paragraphs.english: After three months, the factory worker brings a big red flower. The coach brings a small purple flower. But the poor fisherman brings nothing. He is very sad.
paragraphs.chinese: 三个月后，工厂工人带来了一朵大红花。教练带来了一朵小紫花。但是可怜的渔夫什么也没带。他很伤心。

paragraphs.english: "I don't know why!" he says. "I watered it every day."
paragraphs.chinese: “我不知道为什么！”他说。“我每天都给它浇水。”

paragraphs.english: "You are honest!" the businessman says. "The seeds are bad and will never sprout! The money is yours."
paragraphs.chinese: “你是诚实的！”商人说。“种子是坏的，永远不会发芽！钱是你的了。”

paragraphs.english: The fisherman is very happy. The other two men feel ashamed and sad.

paragraphs.english:
Hush, little boy, don't say a word.
Papa's going to buy you a little bird.

Papa's going to buy you a big, big ring.

Papa's going to buy you a looking glass.
And if that looking glass falls down,
Well, you'll still be the sweetest boy in town.
paragraphs.chinese:
嘘，小男孩，别说话。
爸爸要去给你买只小鸟。

如果那个戒指在课上丢了，
爸爸要去给你买面镜子。
如果那面镜子掉下来，
噢，你仍然是镇上最可爱的男孩。
